code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of Triple objects into a fluent single sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # mapping: predicate -> (verb phrase, optional noun suffix)
    verb_map = {
        "city": ("in", None),
        "country": ("in", None),
        "state": ("in", None),
        "location": ("in", None),
        "latinName": ("has Latin name", None),
        "nickname": ("is nicknamed", None),
        "rector": ("has rector", None),
        "governmentType": ("has government type", None),
        "hasToItsNortheast": ("has to its northeast", None),
        "dean": ("has dean", None),
        "numberOfStudents": ("has", "students"),
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": ("has", "postgraduate students"),
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": ("has", "undergraduate students"),
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": ("has", "doctoral students"),
        "academicStaffSize": ("has", "academic staff"),
        "established": ("was established in", None),
        "affiliation": ("is affiliated with", None),
        "director": ("has director", None),
        "motto": ("has motto", None),
        "officialSchoolColour": ("has official colour", None),
        "outlookRanking": ("has outlook ranking", None),
        "sportsOffered": ("offers", "sport"),
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": ("was given technical campus status by", None),
        "founder": ("was founded by", None),
        "leader": ("has leader", None),
        "leaderTitle": ("has leader title", None),
        "religion": ("has religion", None),
        "headquarter": ("has headquarters in", None),
        "largestCity": ("has largest city", None),
        "river": ("has river", None),
        "hasToItsWest": ("has to its west", None),
        "anthem": ("has anthem", None),
        "ethnicGroup": ("has ethnic group", None),
        "legislature": ("has legislature", None),
        "sportGoverningBody": ("has sport governing body", None),
        "isPartOf": ("is part of", None),
        "neighboringMunicipality": ("neighbors", None),
        "capital": ("has capital", None),
        "patronSaint": ("has patron saint", None),
        "hasToItsNorthwest": ("has to its northwest", None),
        "campus": ("has campus", None),
        "president": ("has president", None),
        "staff": ("has staff", None),
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": ("has elevation", None),
        "postalCode": ("has postal code", None),
        "longName": ("has long name", None),
    }

    # group triples by their subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        groups[t.subject].append(t)

    # helper to join clauses with commas and an Oxford comma
    def join_clauses(clauses):
        if not clauses:
            return ""
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            return clauses[0]
        return ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    subject_clauses = []
    for subject, items in groups.items():
        parts = []
        # build location phrase first
        locations = [t.object.strip('"') for t in items if t.predicate in ("city", "country", "state", "location")]
        if locations:
            parts.append(f"{subject} is located in " + ", ".join(locations))

        # handle remaining predicates
        for t in items:
            if t.predicate in ("city", "country", "state", "location"):
                continue
            verb, noun = verb_map.get(t.predicate, (None, None))
            obj = t.object.strip('"')
            if verb:
                if noun:
                    phrase = f"{subject} {verb} {obj} {noun}"
                else:
                    phrase = f"{subject} {verb} {obj}"
                parts.append(phrase)
            else:
                parts.append(f"{subject} {t.predicate} {obj}")

        subject_clauses.append(join_clauses(parts))

    # join all subject clauses into a single sentence
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        # combine multiple subject descriptions with a coordinating conjunction
        sentence = " While ".join(subject_clauses) + "."

    # capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: University. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:

(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
